advanced/effect-system.title: Effect System
advanced/effect-system.description: @hex-di/result provides a type-level effect system for tracking, manipulating, and enforcing error types across function boundaries. Effects are the error types (E in Result) — they represent the "side effects" a computation may produce.

advanced/serialization.title: Serialization & Interop
advanced/serialization.description: @hex-di/result provides built-in serialization support and type utilities for integration with other systems.

api/api-reference.title: API Reference
api/api-reference.description: Complete reference of all exports from @hex-di/result, organized by category.

concepts/error-patterns.title: Error Patterns
concepts/error-patterns.description: @hex-di/result provides utilities for creating discriminated error types and ensuring exhaustive error handling.

concepts/option-type.title: The Option Type
concepts/option-type.description: The Option type represents a value that may or may not exist, providing a type-safe alternative to null and undefined.

concepts/result-type.title: The Result Type
concepts/result-type.description: The Result type is the foundation of error handling in @hex-di/result. It represents an operation that can either succeed with a value of type T or fail with an error of type E.

guides/async-results.title: Async Results
guides/async-results.description: ResultAsync provides first-class support for asynchronous operations that can fail, wrapping a Promise> with the same rich API as synchronous Results.

guides/combinators.title: Combinators
guides/combinators.description: Functions for working with multiple Results using different short-circuit and error accumulation strategies.

guides/generators.title: Generators & Do Notation
guides/generators.description: @hex-di/result provides two powerful patterns for sequential operations: generator-based flow with safeTry and Do notation for building up context step-by-step.

guides/tagged-error-handling.title: Tagged Error Handling
guides/tagged-error-handling.description: Handle specific error types by their _tag discriminant, progressively eliminating errors from the union type.

guides/transformations.title: Transformations & Chaining
guides/transformations.description: Result types provide a rich set of methods for transforming values, chaining operations, and handling both success and failure cases elegantly.

index.title: Introduction
index.description: Rust-style Result for TypeScript. Errors as values — no throws, no surprises.
